titlestmt.author: Compiled by Cate Peebles and Meg Rinn

publicationstmt.publisher: Yale Center for British Art, Institutional Archives

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Yale Center for British Art, Institutional Archives

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday February 18, 2025 at 18:00

corpname.part: Yale Center for British Art, Institutional Archives

did.abstract: Collection material pertains to correspondence, exhibition catalogs, art-related publications, brochures, and loose-leaf notebooks of art reproductions that document the life and career of John Baskett, art dealer and close friend of Paul Mellon.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: John Baskett (b. 1930) is a former art dealer and advisor to Paul Mellon who was instrumental in forming Mellon's collection of British art. His father, Thomas Baskett, was a dealer at the art dealership firm Colnaghi. Prior to founding his own art dealership, John Baskett was employed by the British Museum, where he worked in the department of prints and drawings; he also assisted his father as a dealer at Colnaghi. In 1961, Baskett took a two-year leave of absence from Colnaghi to assist Paul Mellon at his home in Virginia as curator of Mellon's growing collection of British art.

Baskett served as Paul Mellon's first curator at the Brick House in Virginia (1961–63), where he also managed, with Willis Van Devanter, the creation of an art reference library. In 1967 Baskett received a loan from Paul Mellon, allowing him to found John Baskett Ltd., which was in operation until 1990. Throughout these years he was an influential art advisor to Paul Mellon.
John Baskett served as co-author of Paul Mellon's memoir, Reflections in a Silver Spoon (1992), and maintained a close friendship with Paul Mellon until Mellon's death in 1999.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection contains correspondence, exhibition catalogs, art-related publications, brochures, and loose-leaf notebooks of art reproductions that document the life and career of John Baskett, art dealer and close friend of Paul Mellon.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This collection is arranged into seven series: Series I. Correspondence, 1962 - 2019; Series II. John Baskett, Limited, 1967 - 1990; Series III. Notebooks, undated; Series IV. Photographs, 1961 - 2007; Series V. Publications, 1915 - 2011; Series VI. Subject files, undated, 1995-2005; Series VII. Paul Mellon's funeral and obituaries [restricted], undated, 1999.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to Series I folder 1, Paul Mellon correspondence with Basil Taylor, is restricted. Access to series VII, Paul Mellon's funeral and obituaries, requires permission. Please contact the Yale Center for British Art Archives for more information.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: John Baskett papers. Yale Center for British Art, Archives.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I primarily contains correspondence between John Baskett and Paul Mellon regarding the purchase of art for Mellon's collection. Also included is some correspondence between Baskett and various assistants, curators, and librarians at Mellon's Oak Spring residence. There is also a folder of reproduced correspondence between Baskett, Mellon, and Basil Taylor from the 1960s.

did.unittitle: Paul Mellon to and from Basil Taylor (copies)

originalsloc.head: Existence and Location of Originals
originalsloc.p: Originals are held in the Paul Mellon Collection of Basil Taylor Papers (S006).

Portion of letter from Raymond Seitz

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II, John Baskett, Limited, contains a bound ledger that documents all the sales made by John Baskett during his tenure as an independent art dealer (1967-1990).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series III, Notebooks, contains loose-leaf pages from several notebooks created by John Baskett circa 1958, to learn about prints and drawings, circa the mid-1950s through the early 1960s. Pages include images and description of artworks.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The notebook pages have been kept in their original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series IV, Photographs, includes both print and digitized photographs. The sub-series, Digitized Photographs, contains digitized copies of 272 photographs from an album documenting Baskett's years at the Brick House in Virginia (1961-1963). There are individual images and digitizations of album pages. The album includes photographs of John Baskett and others involved in caring for and building Paul Mellon's art and rare book collections, such as Willis Van Devanter, Martha Tross and Basil Taylor; there are also numerous photographs of the landscape and homes in Upperville, Virginia.

The sub-series in 'Photographs: reproductions included in 'Paul Mellon's Legacy: A Passion for British Art'' contains printed photo reproductions of earier images used to illustrate John Baskett's essay 'Paul Mellon: A Remembrance' in the 2007 catalogue, 'Paul Mellon's Legacy: A Passion for British Art.' These images show Andrew Mellon, Paul Mellon at various stages in his life, and pictures of John Baskett and Paul Mellon together.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Photographs in sub-series 'Digitized album' have been kept in their original order.
Photographs in sub-series 'Photographs: reproductions included in 'Paul Mellon's Legacy: A Passion for British Art' are arranged chronologically.

did.unittitle: John Basket digitized photo album pages, JPEGS

Paul Mellon en route to Omaha Beach, 1944
Paul Mellon with John Baskett at a loan meet at Oak Spring, 1998
Portrait of Basil Taylor by Gerald Frankl, 1952

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series V, Publications, contains an assortment of volumes collected by John Baskett and includes auction catalogues, books about Paul Mellon's collection, and general exhibition catalogues. It also includes pamphlets and brochures relating to Paul Mellon, the Mellon family, and events.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Publications are arranged alphabetically by title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series VI features additional subject files related to John Baskett's work as an art dealer and as advisor to Paul Mellon.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged into two subseries: funeral planning and obituaries. Both are arranged chronologically.

did.unittitle: Condition report on 'Still Life' by Fede Galizia
National Sporting Library Newsletter: "Thomas Rowlandson Drawings from the Paul Mellon Collection"
Postcards from The National Childbirth Trust of Great Britain
The Royal Veterinary College: Save Duncan's Horses Appeal, commemorative brochures
Series VII. Paul Mellon's Funeral and Obituaries

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to Series VII, Paul Mellon's funeral and obituaries requires permission. Please contact the Yale Center for British Art Archives for more information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series VII, Paul Mellon's funeral plans and obituaries, contains material related to the planning of Paul Mellon's funerary services, as well as obituaries published after his death.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged into two subseries: funeral planning and obituaries. Both subseries are arranged chronologically.
